items.hook: Sam Neill’s final films tell us a lot about who he was as an actor – and why we’ll miss him | Luke Buckmaster
items.body: A character actor in a leading man’s body, he gave balls-to-the-wall performances in a subversively varied career
Paul Urkijo Alijo draws on Basque folklore and oral tradition to deliver a forbidden love story transcending reality

items.hook: A Conversation with Oscar Wilde is an outdoor sculpture by Maggi Hambling on Adelaide Street in central London dedicated to Oscar Wilde.
items.body: Unveiled in 1998, it takes the form of a bench-like green granite sarcophagus, with a bust of Wilde emerging from the upper end, with a hand clasping a cigarette. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Carmelopodus is an ichnogenus of theropod dinosaur footprint.
items.body: They are suggested to belong to basal ceratosaurs, due to their similarities with abelisaurid footprints. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Review: Homo Sive Natura - Karlovy Vary 2026 - Proxima
items.body: Giovanni C Lorusso turns a Cambodian land grab into a compelling, visually rigorous study of colonialism surviving long after the colonisers themselves have disappeared

items.hook: California leads lawsuit to block Paramount Warner Bros mega merger
items.body: The state, where both studios have headquarters, has joined 11 others to halt the deal.

items.hook: Lyndhurst is a village and civil parish situated in the New Forest in Hampshire, England, about nine miles (14 km) south-west of Southampton.
items.body: Known as the "capital of the New Forest", Lyndhurst houses the New Forest District Council and Court of Verderers. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Synthetic Sincerity review – Marc Isaacs’ AI interrogation grapples with identity and existence
items.body:
A combination of fact and fiction leaves the celebrated documentarian’s puzzling project about software training wanting for depth

Marc Isaacs’ new film is a curious, intriguing, semi-sincere affair that I couldn’t make friends wit

items.hook: It is situated about 3 miles (4.8 km) south of Maidenhead and 5 miles (8 km) west of Windsor and lies on the border of Bray and Waltham parishes.
items.body: Touchen End, formerly written Touchen-end, is a village in the civil parish of Bray in the English county of Berkshire. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Reverend George Herbert Carpenter (1865–1939) was a British naturalist and entomologist, born in the Peckham district of southeast London in 1865, and died in Belfast on 22 January 1939.
items.body: His main interests were in the study of insects and arachnids, zoogeography, and economic zoology. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Veteran Hong Kong producer Nansun Shi, with credits including 'Infernal Affairs', dies at 75
items.body: Nansun Shi, the Hong Kong producer and co-founder of Film Workshop, has died, age 75.

items.hook: Victoria Eugenia Villarruel is an Argentine politician, lawyer, writer, and activist who has served as the 38th vice president of Argentina since 2023.
items.body: Described as a conservative politician, she is the founder of the civil association Centro de Estudios Legales sobre el Terrorismo y sus Víctimas, which she has chaired since its inception. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Amber Davies calls out audience member for filming Legally Blonde performance
items.body: The ex-Love Islander says she asked for the theatregoer, who was sitting in the front row, to be kicked out.
